system.description:
CLASS DICE: D20

Mastery

You start each session with Mastery. While you have it, you can add your D20 when casting spells. If you do, the dice roll includes the 20-special (activated on a 20).

20-Special: this success counts as two successes.

This is a role-playing game. Yes, you see a world around you... but you know there's rules behind it. And someone running it. Ideally, one day it's going to be you. Until then, you're creeping around the edges of the game, seeing what you can get away with.

Most people look at you and think "wizard". Pfft! Wizards cast spells. You mess with the rules to get what's possible and outright cheat when you need the impossible. The world is a game. That means it's your plaything.

Until someone notices you.

Play the Master if you want to...

  • Re-arrange rules to create your own custom magic spells.
  • Outright cheat when you need something literally impossible.
  • Beg for your life from judgemental extradimensional beings when you get caught.
system.coreNature:
This is a role-playing game. Yes, you see a world around you... but you know there's rules behind it. And someone running it. Ideally, one day it's going to be you. Until then, you're creeping around the edges of the game, seeing what you can get away with.

Most people look at you and think "wizard". Pfft! Wizards cast spells. You mess with the rules to get what's possible and outright cheat when you need the impossible. The world is a game. That means it's your plaything.

Until someone notices you.

PLAY THE MASTER IF YOU WANT TO...

  • Re-arrange rules to create your own custom magic spells.
  • Outright cheat when you need something literally impossible.
  • Beg for your life from judgemental extradimensional beings when you get caught.

coreAbility.name: MASTERY
coreAbility.description:
You start each session with Mastery. While you have it, you can add your D20 when casting spells. If you do, the dice roll includes the 20-special.

20-Special: this success counts as two successes.

looks.name: Wizard robes straight out of a 1980s kids fantasy cartoon
Killer suits straight off a 1980s corporate bad guy
Fetish-wear straight out of a 1990s vampire LARP

specials.name: 20-Special
specials.description: This success counts as two successes

fields.label: Has Mastery
fields.description: Track whether you currently have Mastery. Refreshes at the start of each session.
fields.hint: If lost twice in a session, the Powers That Be deliver punishment.

fields.label: Mastery Lost This Session
fields.description: Track if you've already lost Mastery once this session.
fields.hint: If you lose Mastery again while this is true, face the Powers That Be.

fields.label: My Game, My Rules
fields.initial:
"

Select three Rules – elements of reality you can manipulate with magic. You have power over these rules, and you do not suffer risk when you add them to spells.

Casting Spells

Combine as many rules as needed for your effect. Calculate Risk:

  • For every rule you don't control: +5 Risk
  • For every effect slightly more than standard: +1 Risk
  • For every effect massively more than standard: +5 Risk

If Risk > 20, spell cannot be cast. Otherwise, roll Int pool with Mastery D20:

  • D20 > Risk: No consequences
  • D20 ≤ Risk: Caught by Powers That Be. Lose Mastery.
"

fields.label: My Rules (Choose 3 to start)
fields.description: Rules you control do not add Risk to your spells.
choices: Range - Effect reaches medium range
Area - Affect all within melee range of target
Duration - Lasts for an encounter
Selectivity - Remove yourself from spell effects
Component (Negative) - Requires rare thematic component
Blood Cost (Negative) - Causes 1 Wound to caster or target
Taint of Damnation (Negative) - Disadvantage on social checks
Secrets (Negative) - Must share meaningful secret
Damage: Duration - Special: ignite, 1 hit/round
Damage: Debilitate - Special: Disadvantage on next action
Damage: Paralyse - Special: immobilize if Willpower ≤4
Damage: Chain - Special: hit another target nearby
Damage: Curse - Special: Disadvantage on next action
Damage: Barrage - Double Special: inflict 2 hits
Damage: Bane - Special: 2 hits vs chosen creature type
Damage: Precise - Special: bypass Guard
Heal - Heal 1 Health. Special: +1 Health
Invisibility - Target invisible until hostile action
Counterspell - Cancel ongoing magical effect
Enhance - Add Advantage on defined task/stat
Hinder - Add Disadvantage on defined task/stat
Illusion - Object appears as something else
Manipulate Mechanism - Single mechanism, single action
Detect - Notice presence of a noun (magic, death, etc)
Create Wall - Object with 2×successes Health, 1 hour
Create Obstacle - Difficult terrain, stat roll to traverse
Sleep - Special: sleep if Willpower ≤4
Understand - Comprehend a noun (language, animal, dead)
Create Creature - Summon being (2/2/4/0 stats)
Create Intelligence - Add sapient mind to creation
Exchange - Move 1 stat point between two stats
Intangibility - Move through objects
Levitation - Move freely in all directions
Speed - Move at horse speed
Telepathy - Share thoughts/vision with caster

fields.label: Hardcore Cheating Style
fields.description: Your style determines what kind of cheats you can perform.
choices: Gamist - Mess with rules in this book (e.g., players don't die at 0 health)
Simulationist - Mess with physical laws (e.g., shift gravity, summon storms)
Narrativist - Mess with the story (e.g., right stranger at right time)

fields.label: Hardcore Cheating
fields.initial:
"

You can break the laws of the universe entirely. To cheat:

  1. Say what you want to do
  2. GM describes punishment if caught
  3. If you continue, hide the D20 in one of two containers
  4. GM guesses which container
  • GM misses: Effect happens, you get away with it
  • GM finds it: A Trial begins

A Trial

The Powers That Be appear, fully aware of your transgression. Prepare to beg for your life, make excuses, or justify yourself quickly.

"

fields.label: Spells Known
fields.description: Spells you have memorized or crafted from your rules.

fields.label: New Rule
fields.description: Pick a new Rule from the Rules list. Your understanding of reality's fundamental forces grows ever-deeper.

fields.label: Slow and Steady
fields.description: Between adventures, you may perform a Hardcore Cheat without being caught. This cannot be at the Outrageous level.

fields.label: Reading the Rules
fields.description:
When entering an environment under another Master's power, you're aware of it.

  • Int pool: Ask GM one question per success about the realm
  • Wis pool: Ask GM one question per success about the Master

Fail: Alert the Master. Critical fail: Hit a trap - Master attacks you with a spell.

fields.label: Further Mastery
fields.description: Pick one ability, or gain a New Rule:
fields: {"key":"furtherMasteryChoice","type":"select","label":"Choice","choices":["New Rule","A Great Rule - One rule can be pushed slightly without Risk","Extra Mastery (Once) - Two stacks of Mastery per session","Multimaster - Second Master type at Minor cheats"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}
{"key":"greatRuleSelected","type":"text","label":"Great Rule (if chosen)","initial":"","hint":"Which rule can be pushed without Risk?","description":""}
{"key":"multimasterDomains","type":"html","label":"Multimaster Domains","initial":"","hint":"Track your domains and their cheat tiers.","description":""}

fields.label: A Dungeon of One's Own
fields.description: Create your own dungeon in a pocket dimension. You have Mastery over it and can use Realm Master cheats within.
fields: {"key":"dungeonAccess","type":"select","label":"How is it accessed?","choices":["Via a real doorway (Pro: invitation only, Con: fixed in place)","Via a small magical object (Pro: portable, Con: can be taken)","Via magical words/methods (Pro/Con: anyone who knows can access)"],"initial":"","hint":"","description":""}
{"key":"dungeonRooms","type":"number","label":"Number of Rooms","initial":1,"hint":"Up to your level. Each square = 10ft cube.","description":""}
{"key":"dungeonMap","type":"html","label":"Dungeon Layout","initial":"","hint":"Describe your dungeon's layout and features.","description":""}

fields.label: To the Dungeon!
fields.description: Teleport anywhere in any Realm you have mastery of, or one whose Master has given permission. Can bring a party-worth of people.

fields.label: Realm Mastery
fields.description: Become Master of a region of Die. Within your region, gain Realm Master Hardcore Cheats. Also pick a Further Mastery ability.

fields.label: The Guide (Writing the Rules)
fields.description: Add a whole new realm to the world. Requires being a Realm Master. Roll Int pool with D20: for each success, define one thing found there and what prevents people taking it.
fields: {"key":"newRealmTreasures","type":"html","label":"Things Found There (per success)","initial":"","hint":"List each treasure and what prevents taking it.","description":""}

fields.label: The Manual (Writing the Rules)
fields.description: Add a whole new monstrous species to the world. They are a Great Power. Roll Int pool with D20: one major faction per success.

fields.label: The Handbook (Writing the Rules)
fields.description: Add a whole new sort of adventurer to the world. Roll Int pool with D20: their greatest band has one member per success. They can perform missions via Compressed Adventure Rules.

fields.label: The Arcana (Writing the Rules)
fields.description: Add a whole new form of magic to the world. Create a new Rule for it. Roll Int pool with D20: select one extra Rule per success with reduced Risk thresholds.
fields: {"key":"bonusRules","type":"html","label":"Bonus Rules (per success)","initial":"","hint":"These have reduced Risk thresholds.","description":""}

fields.label: Grandmaster (Level 20)
fields.description: You become all-powerful for exactly one second. Then you are consumed by DIE, forever.
fields: {"key":"grandmasterAction","type":"html","label":"What did you do with your one second of omnipotence?","initial":"","hint":"","description":""}
